a.question: The XRD patterns of which samples are shown here?
a.answer: The in-plane 2θχ and out-of-plane θ−2θ measurements of the samples 100cLi−MnO2, 200cLi-MnO2, 300cLi−MnO2.

a.question: How does lithiation affect the 2θ positions of the main reflections in Figure 5?
a.answer: They shift to lower 2θ values as lithium concentration increases.

a.question: What is the relationship between the d-spacings of the two main reflections in the lithiated samples?
a.answer: The higher-angle reflection has half the d-spacing of the lower-angle reflection.

b.question: How does an increase in lithium content (with an increasing number of lithium ALD cycles) affect the material properties?
b.answer: As the number of lithium ALD cycles increases, so too does the Li content, which leads to changes in the crystalline phase. The reflection at around 42 °2θ, which corresponds to the beta-MnO2 phase, completely disappears when going from 100 to 200 ALD cycles even before the structure has reached stoichiometric LiMn2O4.
